content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
July 18,
1996
Richard Halberg
Assistant Director
Department of Information Technology
915 Capitol Mall, Suite 312
Sacramento, California
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-l80
Dear Mr. Halberg:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
John Thomas Flynn, the Chief Information Officer for the
CA
Department of Information Technology, regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
May Mr.
Flynn serve on the editorial advisory board of ClO
magazine?
CONCLUSION
Under the Act, Mr.
Flynn may serve on the editorial advisory
board of CIO magazine.
Since he will not receive any compensation
for serving on the advisory board,
CIO magazine is not a financial
interest of his.
However, if, at a later time,
Mr.
Flynn should
receive any compensation or gifts, dO may become a financial
interest and the conflict-of-interest provisions may apply.
FACTS
Mr.
Flynn is California’s first Chief Information Officer
and,
as such, is statutorily charged with developing policies
governing the state’s acquisition, deployment and management of
information technology,
including telecommunications technology.
The authority of the Department of Information Technology extends
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.


File No.
A—96—180
Page
2
throughout state governiaent, with the exceptions of the
Legislature, Courts,
and higher education institutions.
Mr.
Flynn has been asked to be a member of the editorial
advisory board of dO magazine.
dO magazine is a nationally
distributed publication which covers technology issues as they
affect large organizations.
As a member of the editorial advisory
board,
Mr.
Flynn would have interaction with members of the
editorial staff approximately every other month to discuss issues
facing chief information officers and emerging trends in
information technology.
Mr.
Flynn would not have any interaction
with the “business” executives of the magazine, nor would he
receive compensation for his service on the advisory board.
In return for his input,
Mr.
Flynn would gain access to
insiders at do who can provide additional information, reference
material, contacts or faxes of past material.
He will also
receive survey results and a copy of the editorial calendar.
According to our telephone conversation, none of these things have
a monetary value.
You also stated that the magazine has offered
Mr.
Flynn a do logo gift, which will not exceed $100 in value.
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat
ing in, or using his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest.
(Section 87100.)
A public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the
official or a member of his immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dol
lars
($250)
or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.


File No.
A—96—180
Page
3
(e)
Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
The amount of the value of
gifts specified by this subdivision shall be
adjusted biennially by the Commission to equal the
same amount determined by the Commission pursuant
to subdivision
(d)
of Section 89504.
(Section 87103(a)—(e).)
As the Chief Information Officer for the
CA Department of
Information Technology,
Mr.
Flynn may not participate in any
governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect on any of his
financial interests.
Under the Act, serving on the advisory board does not
constitute a conflict of interest.
A conflict of interest arises
when a public official participates in a governmental decision
which will materially affect a financial interest.
However,
please consult with the Attorney General’s Office as to whether
serving on the advisory board would be an issue under the doctrine
of incompatible activities.
Under the facts that you have presented, dO magazine is not
a financial interest of Mr.
Flynn’s because he is not receiving
any compensation for serving as a member of the editorial advisory
board and the value of the dO logo gift is less than $100.
Therefore, at the present time,
Mr.
Flynn would not have a
conflict of interest if he were to participate in a governmental
decision regarding ClO magazine.
Please be advised that a designated employee of a state
agency may not accept gifts from any single source in a calendar
year with a total aggregate value of more than $280 if the
employee is required to report the receipt of income or gifts from
that source on his statement of economic interests.
(Section
89503(c).)
Additionally, gifts with an aggregate value of $50 or
more received from a single source during the reporting period
must be disclosed.


File No.
A—96—180
Page
4
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
J11
3
11
S
cher
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:JS:ak
